bullets.text: A Canadian man, Kenneth Law, has admitted to selling toxic chemicals online to individuals globally, leading to charges of aiding suicides. Families in the UK are seeking further charges against him related to 79 deaths.
A dormitory fire at a girls' school in Kenya has resulted in the deaths of at least 16 students, with eight students arrested in connection with the suspected arson attack.

The World Health Organization (WHO) has reported a 'huge' 30-50% death rate in the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and is calling for a ceasefire among armed groups to facilitate health efforts.
The use of AI-generated images by a Victorian state MP has sparked a debate on the role of artificial intelligence in politics.
Australians are currently limited to a 'keyhole view' in understanding any intelligence gaps or government failings that may have preceded the Bondi massacre.
The government is facing scrutiny over its struggle to pass ambitious tax changes through parliament, with ongoing debate about their impact.
The Bank of England governor, Andrew Bailey, indicated that interest rate cuts are contingent on policymakers gaining greater confidence, as a Middle East ceasefire would still introduce uncertainty.
US stocks achieved a historic weekly run, with the S&P 500 reaching a new record, driven by hopes for a ceasefire deal with Iran and continued excitement over the artificial intelligence trade.

A new source of fluctuations in inflation inequality has been identified, where rising upstream input costs lead to similar absolute price increases across varieties within a product category, potentially undermeasuring inflation for lower earners.
